hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between shell 1 and shell 2 elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-adsorbate systems beyond single-atom adsorbates, because charge transfer effects at the active site are governed by inter-shell electronegativity gradients that the current periodic table representation encodes only implicitly through spatial proximity rather than directly as a learnable chemical descriptor.
